title: Writable oci hooks directory for CoreOS
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: # **kind feature**

**Description**

When using podman in CoreOS I want to be able to install runtime hooks (prestart, poststart) into the
filesystem. Currently podman only reads hook json files from ` /usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d`, which is not writable. 

Is it possible to create a new directory that would be writable e.g. `/etc/share/containers/oci/hooks.d` and make podman aware of it?

comments.body:
Running podman (0.8.4-dev) with log-level=debug, I only see that hook description files are read from 

`DEBU[0000] reading hooks from /usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d`

I moved all preinstalled hooks to `/etc/containers/oci/hooks.d` none of them was read nor executed
(register-machine, systemd-hook, umount). 

Do I need a newer version?
Probably need next weeks 0.9.3, once the above patch gets merged.

`podman version 0.9.3-dev` is reading hook description files from `/usr` and `/etc`

````
DEBU[0000] reading hooks from /usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d 
DEBU[0000] reading hooks from /etc/containers/oci/hooks.d 
DEBU[0000] added hook /etc/containers/oci/hooks.d/oci-register-machine.json 
DEBU[0000] added hook /etc/containers/oci/hooks.d/oci-systemd-hook.json 
DEBU[0000] added hook /etc/containers/oci/hooks.d/oci-umount.json 
DEBU[0000] hook oci-register-machine.json did not match 
DEBU[0000] hook oci-systemd-hook.json did not match     
DEBU[0000] hook oci-umount.json did not match           
````

Thanks, 

/cc @jeremyeder
